poem:
**Night Watch**

At one a.m. the doors  
flowered with guns.  
Barnet, Harrow, Watford:  
names like prayers broken.

Men taken for looking—  
for the eye's trespass  
on synagogues,  
on the frail walk home.

We live by counter-light,  
MI5's long vigil;  
yet the real dread  
is the old one:  
that hatred needs no passport.
prompt: You are the poet RS Thomas. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Four men arrested suspected of spying for Iran in south-east England Armed police have arrested four men suspected of spying for Iran and allegedly targeting synagogues as well as Jewish individuals in London. Counter-terrorism detectives are investigating why the alleged surveillance of Jewish locations and individuals was being carried out, and whether it was linked to a wish to carry out attacks on British soil. Part of the investigation is looking at claims in-person surveillance in London took place and whether it was directed from overseas. The operation by counter-terrorism police and MI5 had been going on for months. The arrests of the four men – one Iranian and three dual British/Iranian nationals – were made under the National Security Act and are the first of suspects alleged to be acting in Iran’s interests since the country was attacked by the US and Israel. Three of the arrests were carried out in London and one in Hertfordshire. Shabana Mahmood, the home secretary, said the arrests meant police had kept “Britain safe from a potential threat”. Police said those arrested were a 40-year-old man and a 55-year-old man who were detained at addresses in Barnet, north London. In Harrow, west London, a 22-year-old man was arrested, while a man aged 52 was arrested in Watford. The arrests were carried out at 1am on Friday morning. Police said that at the address in Harrow, six men were also detained for assisting an offender. Those arrested were aged 29, 39, 42, 49 and". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
